slides.headline: Your body is built to move.
slides.body: You have over 600 muscles in your body. Physical therapy helps retrain the ones that forget how to work together after injury, surgery, or long periods of inactivity.

slides.headline: Walking 30 minutes a day can reduce fall risk by up to 23%.
slides.body: Regular movement is one of the most powerful medicines we have — and your PT team is here to help you move safely and confidently.

slides.headline: Ice or heat — which is right?
slides.body:
Ice is best in the first 48–72 hours after an injury to reduce swelling.

Heat works better after that — relaxing muscles and improving circulation. When in doubt, ask your PT!

slides.headline: PT has been healing people since WWI.
slides.body: Physical therapy was first widely used to rehabilitate soldiers returning from World War I — and it has been growing ever since. Today, PTs work in hospitals, schools, sports facilities, and homes.

slides.headline: Your hand is an engineering marvel.
slides.body: Each hand contains 27 bones, 29 joints, and over 30 muscles. Occupational therapy specializes in helping all of them work together for everyday tasks.

slides.headline: OT helps you get back to the things that matter most.
slides.body: Cooking. Driving. Getting dressed. Playing with grandchildren. Occupational therapy is about returning to the activities that make your life yours.

slides.headline: "Occupation" means more than work.
slides.body: In occupational therapy, "occupation" refers to any meaningful activity — from brushing your teeth in the morning to playing a musical instrument. If it matters to you, OT can help.

slides.headline: OT goes far beyond injury recovery.
slides.body: Occupational therapy also supports people with stroke recovery, developmental delays, autism, chronic pain, and aging-related challenges — helping people of all ages live more independently.

slides.headline: The human voice is extraordinary.
slides.body: The human voice can produce over 100 different phonetic sounds across all the world's languages — and speech-language pathologists are experts in how all of them work.

slides.headline: Speech therapy is about much more than how you speak.
slides.body: Speech-language pathologists also treat swallowing difficulties, memory challenges, voice disorders, and communication after stroke or brain injury.

slides.headline: The brain can rewire itself.
slides.body: After a stroke, many patients regain speech ability through neuroplasticity — the brain's remarkable ability to form new pathways. Speech therapy is one of the most powerful tools for making that happen.

slides.headline: 1 in 12 children has a speech or language disorder.
slides.body: Speech-language pathologists work with children and adults alike — helping kids develop communication skills and helping adults rebuild them after illness or injury.

slides.headline: Cardiac rehab is one of the most effective treatments available.
slides.body: Completing a cardiac rehab program can reduce the risk of future heart events by up to 30% — and it's covered by most insurance plans.

slides.headline: Your heart beats 100,000 times every single day.
slides.body: Cardiac rehab helps make every one of those beats more efficient — improving your heart's strength, endurance, and resilience over time.

slides.headline: Cardiac rehab is about more than exercise.
slides.body: A complete program includes supervised exercise, nutrition guidance, stress management, and medication education — giving your heart every advantage for a strong recovery.

slides.headline: Most eligible patients don't enroll — but they should.
slides.body: Only about 20–30% of eligible heart patients enroll in cardiac rehab, even though it is one of the most proven, effective treatments in cardiovascular medicine. Ask your care team if it's right for you.

slides.headline: Small weight changes make a big difference.
slides.body: For every pound of body weight lost, approximately 4 pounds of pressure is removed from your knees with each step. Small changes can mean significant relief.

slides.headline: Most orthopedic problems don't require surgery.
slides.body: Physical therapy, targeted injections, and lifestyle changes resolve the majority of orthopedic cases. Your care team always explores every non-surgical option first.

slides.headline: Bone is alive — and always rebuilding.
slides.body: Bone is living tissue that constantly remodels itself throughout your life. That's why the right activity, nutrition, and treatment can make a real difference at any age.

slides.headline: Cartilage has no blood supply.
slides.body: Unlike muscle, cartilage receives no direct blood supply — which is why joint injuries tend to heal more slowly. Your orthopedic team understands this biology and plans treatment accordingly.
